sections.title: What is a sales order?
sections.body: A sales order is a document a seller creates to confirm a customer's order after a quotation is accepted. It records the agreed items, quantities, prices, taxes and delivery terms, and it becomes the internal instruction to fulfil and then bill the order. It sits between the quotation and the invoice, giving you a clean record of what was promised before anything ships.

sections.title: Confirm, fulfil, invoice
sections.body: Paper & Pen connects the sales order to the whole flow so an accepted quote becomes a fulfilled, invoiced order without re-keying.
items: Convert an accepted quotation into a sales order, or create one directly
Collect a deposit and track the balance
Fulfil the order with a delivery note
Convert to an invoice in one click
See order status: pending, partially delivered, complete, invoiced

sections.title: Quote to cash, connected
sections.body: Quotation, sales order, delivery note, invoice, payment, and credit note if needed. Paper & Pen links every step so each document carries forward into the next, the totals always reconcile, and your accounts update as the order progresses. Nothing is typed twice and nothing falls through the gaps.

faqs.q: What is the difference between a sales order and an invoice?
faqs.a: A sales order confirms what the customer agreed to buy and is the instruction to fulfil it. An invoice requests payment once goods or services are delivered. You convert one to the other in one click.
Yes. Creating, tracking and fulfilling sales orders is free forever on Paper & Pen.

faqs.q: Can I take a deposit on a sales order?
faqs.a: Yes. Collect a deposit against the order and track the outstanding balance until it is invoiced and settled.

faqs.q: Can I convert a quote to a sales order?
faqs.a: Yes. An accepted quotation converts to a sales order in one click, carrying every line, discount and tax across.

faqs.q: How do I fulfil a sales order?
faqs.a: Create a delivery note from the sales order to ship the goods, then convert to an invoice. Fulfilment status updates as you go.
